messages.content:
Format the following passage properly, for storytelling purposes.

To shift gears for a moment--which, as you will soon find out, is exceptionally long relative to what most consider a 'moment' to be--I cannot do justice to my story without elaborating on my experiences living as an autistic man in a world whose culture was shaped by the 'neurotypical', functioning for their benefit. Whereas a neurologically 'normal' individual has a well balanced proficiency in terms of being able to use both hemispheres of their brain, the autistic individual will be far above average in one hemisphere, while being woefully inefficient in the other. I possess what would today be given a generic classification under 'high functioning autism' though in my youth it was labeled as Asperger's syndrome, a term which some folks still use today, albeit primarily those of older generations. My childhood was an interesting one, from the standpoint that while I did go outside and 'touch grass', by and large I spent a lot of time indoors. As it tends to be with most children, I absorbed various forms of media, including television (naturally, animated series and films were mainstays on our 'idiot boxes'), video games (I grew up with the PlayStation 1 and N64 and still consider them the best consoles ever made; while the technology is by orders of magnitude superior now to what we had back then, I feel the developers of today do less with more), and music (digested mainly in the form of cassettes and CDs; I gained an especially profound affinity for classical, new age and jazz). While I didn't get as immersed in them as I could have been, I did enjoy books too. In my youth, the library was my second favorite place in the world to be. A library card was a gateway to realms far beyond mortal comprehension, and if the nearest public library were closer to my house, I would have been there every day. Though I was too young to fully grasp the significance, I was drawn to the writings of ancient philosophers and would soak up Shakespeare like a sponge. Even at a very young age, I had a profound love for the arts. I was spellbound on the occasions where I got to see a play, or go to a concert. There is something so organic about a live performance, the idea that your energy can be given to a performer just as they can give their energy to you. You can listen to a CD a thousand times and the recordings on that disc will never sound any different, but no two concerts will ever sound exactly the same, even if the performances take place only hours apart and the setlists are identical. The participation and spirit of the audience can turn a good performance into a great one, a great one into a historic one. This immersion proved consequential from the standpoint that it somewhat stunted my social development, and while those with autism typically face uphill battles from the beginning with regards to socialization, I got off to an especially bad start even by those standards. I didn't say my first word until I was three, when I was looking at a Sunday morning newspaper and randomly blurted out 'Natuzzi', pronouncing it as nuh-tootsie (which was almost right, but not quite). I had issues with not knowing how to calm down when something or someone got me upset, and this resulted in a few awkward moments (including someone kicking me in my butt and then running away, with me chasing after them and eventually breaking down in a strange episode of prolonged crying which made me about ten minutes late for my next class; I'm grateful to be able to say that was actually the worst I was bullied in school). I would sometimes get aggressive, even with my parents, but never drew blood. I did get a spanking every so often, but never a beating. I never bullied a child into giving me lunch money or anything else of interest. The ways in which I process emotions were (and, though to a lesser extent than before, still are) different from what is considered the norm among neurotypicals. For example, there is a story my mother told me about an experience she had shopping at a department store--I'm tempted to say it was a JCPenney, and that it took place sometime between 1994 and 1996--that resulted in a rather embarrassing moment. She brought me with her and all was as you would expect, until I heard the sound of a child crying, which caused me to break into a fit of hysterical laughter. My mother was mortified to look around and see multiple shoppers glaring at her. I don't believe it is possible for a mind to have developed so fully at so young an age as to be consciously predisposed to schadenfreude, notwithstanding the sin element that humans inherit through the transgressions of Adam and Eve in the Garden of Eden, as is taught in the Bible. There are times where my mind wanders into a dimension of dark, even twisted, thoughts, but I have never considered myself to be a natural-born sadist. Having never been the recipient of a proper psychiatric evaluation, years of self-diagnosis and my personal reflections have led me to the conclusion that the laughter I expressed upon hearing the tears of that child was a psychosocial response to an uncomfortable situation. As mentioned at the top of this piece, we live in a world that was shaped by neurotypicals, and one part of how they've shaped it is through the evolution of 'acceptable' social behaviors. Some people will laugh when they hear tears, not necessarily as an intended act of cruelty but as a way to make themselves feel better, or potentially even cheer up the one who is sad. (And of course, there is indeed such a thing as tears of joy.) Just about everything I know now about how I act and react was learned through trial and error. This is why it's important to be a student of life, and a constant observer. The public school system has created many great workers, but precious few great thinkers.

To shift gears for a moment—which, as you will soon find out, is exceptionally long relative to what most consider a 'moment' to be—I cannot do justice to my story without elaborating on my experiences living as an autistic man in a world whose culture was shaped by the 'neurotypical,' functioning for their benefit.

Whereas a neurologically 'normal' individual has a well-balanced proficiency in terms of being able to use both hemispheres of their brain, the autistic individual will be far above average in one hemisphere, while being woefully inefficient in the other. I possess what would today be given a generic classification under 'high-functioning autism,' though in my youth it was labeled as Asperger's syndrome, a term which some folks still use today, albeit primarily those of older generations.

My childhood was an interesting one, from the standpoint that while I did go outside and 'touch grass', by and large, I spent a lot of time indoors. As it tends to be with most children, I absorbed various forms of media, including television (naturally, animated series and films were mainstays on our 'idiot boxes'), video games (I grew up with the PlayStation 1 and N64 and still consider them the best consoles ever made; while the technology is by orders of magnitude superior now to what we had back then, I feel the developers of today do less with more), and music (digested mainly in the form of cassettes and CDs; I gained an especially profound affinity for classical, new age, and jazz).

While I didn't get as immersed in them as I could have been, I did enjoy books too. In my youth, the library was my second favorite place in the world to be. A library card was a gateway to realms far beyond mortal comprehension, and if the nearest public library were closer to my house, I would have been there every day.

Though I was too young to fully grasp the significance, I was drawn to the writings of ancient philosophers and would soak up Shakespeare like a sponge. I didn't say my first word until I was three, when I was looking at a Sunday morning newspaper and randomly blurted out 'Natuzzi', pronouncing it as nuh-tootsie (which was almost right, but not quite).

The ways in which I process emotions were (and, though to a lesser extent than before, still are) different from what is considered the norm among neurotypicals. For example, there is a story my mother told me about an experience she had shopping at a department store—I'm tempted to say it was a JCPenney, and that it took place sometime between 1994 and 1996—that resulted in a rather embarrassing moment. She brought me with her and all was as you would expect, until I heard the sound of a child crying, which caused me to break into a fit of hysterical laughter. My mother was mortified to look around and see multiple shoppers glaring at her.

I don't believe it is possible for a mind to have developed so fully at so young an age as to be consciously predisposed to schadenfreude, notwithstanding the sin element that humans inherit through the transgressions of Adam and Eve in the Garden of Eden, as is taught in the Bible. There are times where my mind wanders into a dimension of dark, even twisted, thoughts, but I have never considered myself to be a natural-born sadist. Having never been the recipient of a proper psychiatric evaluation, years of self-diagnosis and my personal reflections have led me to the conclusion that the laughter I expressed upon hearing the tears of that child was a psychosocial response to an uncomfortable situation.

As mentioned at the top of this piece, we live in a world that was shaped by neurotypicals, and one part of how they've shaped it is through the evolution of 'acceptable' social behaviors. Some people will laugh when they hear tears, not necessarily as an intended act of cruelty but as a way to make themselves feel better, or potentially even cheer up the one who is sad. (And of course, there is indeed such a thing as tears of joy.)

Just about everything I know now about how I act and react was learned through trial and error. This is why it's important to be a student of life, and a constant observer. The public school system has created many great workers, but precious few great thinkers.

Create a scam-baiting response to the most recent email, to keep the scammer in a false sense of security by posing as a potential victim.

Email #1, from Cameron (scam-baiter):

Dear Marlon,

I trust this message finds you in the best health and high spirits.

I’d like to express my gratitude once again for considering me a partner in this substantial investment venture. The sum of USD$2.3 Billion for investment is indeed significant and I am cognizant of the gravity of the responsibility that handling such an amount entails.

I am reassured by our mutual understanding that any changes or amendments we may incorporate into our agreement would be intended to refine and affirm our partnership. The finalization of the investment strategy will indeed herald the commencement of a prosperous venture for both of us and potentially for the beneficiaries of our charitable 5%.

I appreciate your patience as I meticulously review each aspect of the investment framework and its alignment with our agreed terms, specifically the allocation of 62.5% to me, and the 32.5% to your side, including the nuanced calculation of management fees and charitable contributions.

Having read your email, now more than ever, I believe that a face-to-face or virtual meeting with the Empower representative before my trip to Amsterdam would be beneficial. This would not only solidify our relationship but also accelerate the expeditious conclusion of our agreement and arrangements.

As per your instructions, I have reviewed our initial stipulations concerning the expense reimbursements and ROI shares. It is clear to me that the 1/2% management fee is a common practice for funds of this magnitude and is necessary to ensure the optimum management and administration of the investment vehicles.

Moreover, I welcome the spiritual angle you have introduced, as it offers a broader perspective on our efforts beyond financial gains.

That said, I have a few additional requests before proceeding further:
- A comprehensive breakdown of the administrative costs that fall under the 1/2% management fee.
- Detailed profiles and credentials of the Empower representative and any other officials who will act on behalf of our partnership.
- Any additional documentation that outlines the roles and responsibilities of each party within the agreement.

In light of the complexity and the large scale of the proposed investment, I’m sure you’ll agree on the importance of thoroughness and clarity moving forward. This will minimize any misunderstandings and set the stage for a clear, transparent, and successful partnership.

I will endeavor to provide feedback on the agreement at my earliest convenience. The removal of any metaphorical stains to ensure the safety and reputation of both parties remains a priority, as our mutual success lies within the meticulous details of our coordination.

I look forward to your guidance on the specifics mentioned above and will readily avail myself for any discussions or meetings necessary to advance to the next phase of our partnership.

May wisdom guide us and fortune favor our plans.

Respectfully,

Cameron McCaffrey

Email #2, from Marlon:

Dear McCaffrey

Good Evening Friend McCaffrey

How are you this moment with your Family.Thank you for your latest response to our interactions about investing funds from Benin. I must admit to being a bit confused in your writing. Language barriers, stilted language can separate us, and for something so important, neither of our groups would want to have any confusion.I have a number of specific questions based upon your latest input,and I am hoping you can be specific in your responses so we both can understand how this would work.  

Once again,, Based on my previous email to you whatever funds should have its remittance on yourself arrival in Amsterdam-Holland and not any other where.So miss mentioned, so that is stated to you with the information's to set up this account for further transfer of the investment funds at your present which even before your arrival the Empower rep who will leave to be there ahead of you and right now from your response while over balance up the agreement amendment I am getting down plans over that concerned for an Empower representative, whereby I am been kind to hold on for you from a back little time to finish up going through the amendment maybe by at any good time that you will round up if you mean mentioned this ((((((((((((( I will endeavor to provide feedback on the agreement at my earliest convenience. The removal of any metaphorical stains to ensure the safety and reputation of both parties remains a priority, as our mutual success lies within the meticulous details of our coordination.))))))))))))). Then Time frame for beginning such this investment package..?????????.

Anyway from what you put out let me get around it and see how the amendment comes to be the best of what you can suggest, because apart from the way you get the mark close, then is the investments administrator's going handle by the 62.5% your side,'so get that sorry for the inconveniences this my response letter may cause you,because you mentioned not anything confusing I think that should be the language I meant even before now.so from my side I have no problem than to hold on for you till everything by your side of the agreement amendment balance up as long as you will remain honest to me till the end of this important clear out agreement amendment balance up. Meanwhile trusting in you and believing in God that you will never let me down either now or in future.

    Some clarification of issues for Cameron McCaffrey. If you mean mentioned this below here 


That said, I have a few additional requests before proceeding further: 

- A comprehensive breakdown of the administrative costs that fall under the 1/2% management fee. )))))

- Detailed profiles and credentials of the Empower representative and any other officials who will act on behalf of our partnership. )))))

- Any additional documentation that outlines the roles and responsibilities of each party within the agreement.)))

1. I mean You had brought up some issue about the ½% annual management fee. You would get ½% each year for managing the funds. You had asked about the split of the R O I. After the points you raised, I suggest we each get 47.5% of the R O I, and that leaves 5% of the R O I to redeploy into good humanitarian items on a continuing basis; this 5% from R O I is separate from 5% of the initial capital which would be available for particular non profit /do gooder organizations. 

Sir. This is a change from the earlier discussions,but seems to be a useful compromise,and yet insures that even 5% of the ROI funds will continue to be used for humanitarian/religious/good public uses. The point you seemed to make is that the ½% would cover all expenses we might have here in handling the funds; to the extent that is true, is fine, but there could be large special projects wherein the legal and administrative costs could be very large in any one year (even equal to the total ½% fee, since some large projects, like $300 million or more, requires lots of due diligence and much legal and travel costs). , and require additional charges, which would be tracked as part of any investment projects.

1 A. My point about income tax is merely that even if the funds are placed in a place without taxes, the investments may go somewhere that does have taxes to be paid on profits. So suppose that investments yielded 4% return at some point, but are all subject to taxes,that could mean the actual return is reduced to 3% or so since it would be mandated…..something like that. We of course all seek better returns, but whatever it is,so there may be some impact; we of course would work to minimize that. and that is much easier to justify.

1 B. The point is, We both seem to have wanted to places for set aside for the poor and good things…First 5% of the remaining capital funds should be used for investment in good things, helping the poor, etc…. Second, 5% of the fund earning s would also be used for good things for the poor and indigent.So Your side would provide general input as appropriate into what non profit tasx exempt organizations should receive such funds; in the absence of such input, I presume we on this end would try to use good judgement to spread it around, for religious, support the poor.

2. I still have much difficulty re reading much of what you say each time; just some fundamental differences in language and meaning, so I try to respond to you, but if I have failed to, it is not because I am ignoring you, but because I just do not know what to say in all that you provide and since it can be so confusing and unclear. Sorry, but just a problem. I mean if you mean mentioned this ((((( - Detailed profiles and credentials of the Empower representative and any other officials who will act on behalf of our partnership. )))))))). Sir I mean Like I mentioned earlier,you shall arrive to meet up with an Empower representative,so know big deal I think I told you that before even your arrival that the Empower representative will be going to be on his arrival ahead of you so that on your arrival you will meet up with the Rep,and so with that of this assurance, not even assurance, but self-confidence, self-assurance, belief in oneself, and this is all what I said to you since I unable to move due to reasons of the duty post I preserve and highly maintain here , so just because of schedules I provide you every cool-headedness condition to keep up. Sorry for the delay in answering your message.

2 A. HOWEVER, I want you to understand this again if you mean mentioned this ((((( and any other officials who will act on behalf of our partnership )))))) So what did you by to understand with this statement from you because I have made it clear to you that this arrangement is only known to you and me. or what did you mean by on what is the group official name that will be receiving such reports.Sir so what did you mean by of the business group or Where exactly are you coming from to understand what is never done any where in the world. McCaffrey Look just to be sure to avoid problems. I want you to be aware of this,since I Marlon indicate that you would meet with my representative in Amsterdam, Let me know for sure you understand this message.

2 A. If you mean mentioned this (( Having read your email,now more than ever,I believe that a face-to-face or virtual meeting with the Empower representative before my trip to Amsterdam would be beneficial.This would not only solidify our relationship but also accelerate the expeditious conclusion of our agreement and arrangements.))))))). Friend McCaffrey I had suggested the main points of difference be highlighted and then alternative solutions be outlined and agreed to before both of us eating up draft. including legal and financial issues; this is a delicate matter that can not be easily ignored. In order to receive and wire funds from  Amsterdam, or is it not the same face to face I had been stated to you and no any need for any other place of having things to do apart from where to move the investment funds out from. so rest assured I have been concerned about our investment funds for best interest of all!!!!!!!

3. So what did you understood before from What form of reporting requirements will there be, if you mean mentioned this ((( Any additional documentation that outlines the roles and responsibilities of each party within the agreement. )))) And what is now concern the Investment Partnership agreement amendment with  what will be added further apart from the resources/funds /security /funding /costs. On the such been that I think have stated it in my back email that the investment funds  facility have been programmed, I mean on your concern by that on your dated email of Thursday,December 14th, 2023.So Rest assured I am not in any way trying to ignore you, but indicate the criticality of doing things carefully, slowly and deliberately, so it is not a matter I think of you to repeat, although maybe I am trying to understand it been that you perhaps wanted to sound it out again if I mean likely it was why you raise it up once again.then I am reviewing also for conformance to other details question, so but I believe just to put you through been past tense. 

In conclusion. Right now so that sound no offend,of which you don't be offend,because no any unfamiliar to it, McCaffrey. well I appreciate you for the watch word to have a successful project, so in full co-operation at least you are very careful on what you wish for, so I think I am clear for over the mistake and not misrepresentation. although I don't need to finger out this of this any more, I mean I regret the misunderstanding. Finally, knowing your arrangements to accomplish this opportunities in a timely manner with my Warm Regards to you. Thank you and God bless you all.

Sincerely
 Marlon,

Dear Marlon,

Hello and warm regards to you and your team. I hope this message meets you well. 

First of all, I want to thank you for the detailed clarification provided in your email. I understand the complexities and am committed to achieving a clear understanding between us despite any language or conceptual barriers we might encounter. Ensuring we have a solid agreement is of the utmost importance for a venture of this magnitude.

Regarding the ½% management fee for the administration of the funds, your explanation made it clear that this should cover most of the everyday expenses tied to the management of our investment. However, I concur with your prudence that for exceptionally large projects, additional costs may be incurred. It is refreshing to deal with someone who is attentive to such details and transparency.

I am fully in agreement with the allocation of 5% of the ROI to be used for humanitarian/charitable purposes. The notion that we are contributing to the greater good even while we seek profit from our investments adds an extra layer of satisfaction to this endeavor. 

In terms of our partnership and the roles we will play, I share your belief in being deliberate and accurate. I would like to reiterate the importance of receiving the profiles and credentials of the Empower representative and any other officials who might have pivotal roles in our partnership.

Additionally, and to reemphasize my previous request, any documentation that reinforces our mutual roles and responsibilities will be beneficial. Without a doubt, the clearer our agreement, the stronger our trust will be.

I also agree with your suggestion that we should have a face-to-face or virtual meeting with the Empower representative prior to my trip to Amsterdam. That shall undoubtedly fortify our partnership and hasten the finalization of our agreement.

Regarding the concern about taxes, your insight is appreciated. Understandably, the net return on investments may fluctuate due to taxes, and it's reassuring to know that efforts will be made to minimize these impacts.

I understand the gravity of our venture and assure you of my commitment to making this a successful and timely project. Please rest assured of my complete honesty and dedication to our agreed objectives.

Once again, thank you for your patience and clarity in this process. I trust that moving forward, we will work harmoniously towards the realization of our shared goals.

God bless you too.

Best wishes,

Cameron McCaffrey

Create a scam-baiting response to the most recent email, to keep the scammer in a false sense of security by posing as a potential victim.

Email #1, from Cameron (scam-baiter):

Dear Mr. <PRESIDIO_ANONYMIZED_PERSON>,

Thank you for reaching out to me with this opportunity in gold and diamond investments in Ghana. I am very interested in learning more about your project and potentially becoming an investor in this lucrative venture.

As a Financial Counselor/Consultant, I understand the importance of reliable partnerships and responsible investments. I appreciate your offer and am eager to hear more details about the mining sites, the process of investing, and the availability of gold for sale.

Please provide me with further information about the project, including any documentation or contracts that may be necessary to proceed with the investment. I am willing to explore this opportunity further and look forward to your response.

Best regards,
Cameron McCaffrey
cmccaff1@fau.edu

Email #2, from George:

COMPREHENSIVE DETAILS.
 

Hello Cameron McCaffrey,

 

Thanks for your kind response to my proposal, thanks for agreeing to cooperate, start and finish the project with us; from the depth of my heart, I appreciate all; my prayers to our Creator is to give us (everyone involved in the project) good & sound health and long lives to see the beginning and the positive results of the project, amen!

 

It is important to let you know that I have made proper arrangements in securing this prospective rights and privilege; the local mining crews are available, the gold land owners are available, the only son (adopted) is also available, the lawyer is available and the rest of my colleagues; you shall be known as the accepted/approved and endorsed foreign investor of this project. Kindly read all about the project and what you are coming to Ghana to do with us below.

 

1.         There are lands of gold to be mined both surface & deep mining; the owners of the lands and the Kings & chiefs have been met and informed about you; they are all expecting your arrival soon, when you arrive, we are going to meet them all.

2.       There was a project financier (but deceased) he was a former bank director who agreed to release (in fact, the funds was released before his sudden death) to us US$155.8Million to start the investment on this mining, his target was to invest the funds fully on local gold miners before his demise, the financier never wanted his name to appear/reflect (in any ways or manners) in the investment due to the source of his wealth but unfortunately, the old man died 18 months ago,  but he has his only young and adopted handicapped son/child as the beneficiary but due to the laws of our land the boy is not allowed to handle such project and funds until he is 45yrs old coupled with his disability; and this is why I am standing in for the deceased old man as his investment consultant and his only child and backed up by the financier’s personal/family lawyer.

3.       This will be between the three of us plus the child, this does not require that you have any experience in gold mining, also, it does not require advance or upfront payments but as a businessman and upon your arrival a little expenses concerning the release of the funds may be required of you plus some miscellaneous expenses you may likely make on your own (this will never cost you fortune, it is advisable to equip yourself with $5,000.00-$8,000.00 but whatever you spent shall be reimbursed to you) and or except you want or you wish to put your funds around the project when and after seeing, meeting and confirming all that I told you about.

4.        You are coming to Ghana as a foreign partner/investor to inspect the project proposed to you, meet with the land owners and the King of the village where the lands are situated, meet with the handicapped boy (beneficiary) and all the people involved.

5.       The project; We want to invest these funds in Africa (Ghana to be precise) on gold mining with the lands of gold in Kibi, Obuasi, Koforidua and Kumasi, all in Ghana, the deceased old man had reached an agreement with the local miners to help them by boosting their productions largely, which the local miners were all happy with but death denied them the privilege since the demise of the old man. The government of our country is willing and ready to issue prospective foreign investor/s license, approval and permit (especially now that that embargo has been lifted on the permit and license & lifting of ban on the Chinese & the Lebanese) to mine gold now; the privilege was once given many years ago but when the Lebanese & Chinese  people abused the privilege given to them the government had to withdraw the licenses because the Lebanese & Chinese people refused to recognize and take care of the local miners that worked on the gold mining sites and the villagers (land owners) all the promises the Lebanese & Chinese people made were fake promises that they never fulfilled it was due to this abuse of privilege that prompted the government of our country to the withdrawal of the license and approvals. Now, they have revisited and reviewed the issue which made them to start giving out licenses to foreign investors strictly by recommendations and partnership with the indigenes, this is where you are safe, secure and backed up in this project.

6.       We all must meet face-face in Kibi, Obuasi, Koforidua and Kumasi, Ghana, a country in West Africa where the gold lands/sites and the funds are, Ghana is a tourist country, highly industrialized and manufacturing country and peaceful in Africa, because this is the country where this man deposited the money in a private security outfit. When the three of us and the child meet here, we shall all discuss, negotiate, plan and conclude on the success of the project, and then the deceased old man’s lawyer and the child will endorse the release of the funds to us.

7.        You shall register a company here in Ghana; the name of the company would be chosen by you, the name of the company to register here should be similar to your company name in your country

8.       Individual and company/corporate bank accounts shall be opened for you on your name and on your full information here in Ghana this is from where the bank (from where bank accounts is created on your name or on your company’s name that you have in your company here in Ghana) will now transfer these funds to your offshore account(s) in your presence and you will call your account managers in your banks in your country or wherever you have your bank accounts for confirmation of the arrival of the funds before you leave Ghana but you must bear in mind that all that we are doing is for the child’s future of which we shall both be compensated and benefited from it, except you want to invest your personal interests in the project.

9.       After the initial transfer, you will be transferring the funds to me for this project anytime I need the funds and with the knowledge of the deceased’s lawyer and the boy but the whole funds will be in your accounts and on your name and with the situational reports each and every time funds is being requested for; you will also need to oversee the project as it progresses as the foreign investor due to our initial plans and agreement. The company and all related formalities about this project will also be on your name. It will look like a foreign investor funding his business here in Africa.

10.      You are coming to adopt the handicapped boy as your son and all the formalities has to be done legally and legitimately, after adoption you will handle the preparation of the travel documents of the boy to come to your country to leave with you as your child and handle all his educational levels and carrier in life.

11.        Finally, a MEMORANDUM OF UNDERSTANDING (MOU) between me and you and the financier’s lawyer’s endorsement who will release the total sum of US$155.8Million to us. The agreement will state the percentages/commission you will have in this project for the assistance/support/efforts (financially & morally) and the percentage/commission you will be having in the company returns for between 50-100 years.

 

Also, expenses incurred for the travelling, phone calls and others will be paid back to you before we get to the middle of the projects; but all invoices, receipts and all forms of payments made by you as expenses incurred in the course of the projects MUST be produced before or when the meeting would be held about the negotiation and reimbursement will take place. Also, I will obtain for you the application form from the Ministry of Mining to register on your name. We are not asking and did not ask you for any advance/upfront payment because of the nature of the projects but when you come down to Kibi, Obuasi, Koforidua and Kumasi, Ghana to the project sites, meet with the team on a round table, discuss, negotiate and conclude, you may choose/decide to put in your funds around the structure/projects but *strictly* on both parties’ agreement and duly documented legally. These projects are all about choice; please have a rethink over this before we fully commence these projects. We also have some kilograms of Gold to sell (outside the projects and from the local miners) but with good prices and lower than the world market rates; if you are interested or have good and reliable buyer/s that can buy from them, please do not hesitate to let me know, we shall compensate you as an agent/broker for that as well. You are free to come down to Ghana with your personal/company lawyer if you so want and if it is convenient for you, there is no problem because this transaction is legal and legitimate; ‘transparency’ is highly required. This project is expected to be commenced and finally executed as quickly as we can but it all depends on your readiness and time. At most, we should commence the negotiation and finalize the project in 1-2 months’ time.

 Would you prefer us to send you a letter of invitation, cable visa or you will sort yourself out about these? The next step would be definitely conveyed to your humble self when and after your mind is made up to fully commence the projects with us.

Please be free to ask questions of what you need to know about; make suggestions and bring in ideas.
